Short Version (~10 words): The first nuclear fission experiment in the US is conducted in Pupin Hall.
Event: The first nuclear fission experiment in the US is conducted in a cyclotron in the basement of Pupin Hall.

Short Version (~10 words): T freshmen steal 25 grams of uranium-238 using Pupin's tunnels
Event: Using the tunnels under Pupin Hall, two freshmen steal 25 grams of uranium-238.

Short Version (~10 words): Joseph Gordon Levitt drops out of Columbia to resume acting career
Event: Joseph Gordon Levitt drops out of Columbia to resume acting career

Short Version (~10 words): Men dressed in Ku Klux Klan robes set fire to a cross on South Lawn.
Event: Twenty men dressed in Ku Klux Klan robes set fire to a 7-foot wooden cross on South Lawn.

Short Version (~10 words): George Washington andJohn Adams attend Commencement Ceremony.
Event: U.S. President George Washington and Vice President John Adams attend the Commencement Ceremony.

Short Version (~10 words): The 116th-Street Columbia University subway station is founded
Event: The 116th-Street Columbia University subway station is founded as part of the New York City Subway line.

Short Version (~10 words): The Student Homophile League is founded at Columbia.
Event: The Student Homophile League, the first gay student organization in the country, is founded at Columbia.

Short Version (~10 words): Barnard becomes the last of the Seven Sisters to accept trans students
Annie Meyer publishes an essay that leads to the formation of Barnard
Event: Annie Meyer publishes a 2,500 word essay in The Nation arguing that New York City culture suffers from the lack of a liberal arts college for women. As a result of ongoing advocacy from herself and her associates, Barnard College is founded one year later.

Short Version (~10 words): The Cheryl and Philip Milstein Center for Teaching and Learning opens at Barnard
Event: The Cheryl and Philip Milstein Center for Teaching and Learning opens at Barnard

Short Version (~10 words): Columbia goes co-ed, the final Ivy Leauge to do so
Event: Columbia goes co-ed, the final Ivy Leauge to do so.
